pr.title: First part of cleaning up incbins/pointers in .data
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly-data cleanup pass that began replacing opaque .data incbins with explicit .4byte/.asciz/.balign data and symbolic pointers. The bulk of the work was in asm/data.s, with many new global labels added in text assembly files so tables in .data could point at real symbols instead of remaining anonymous/raw. There was no PR body or review discussion in the slice, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from the changes.

key_files.role: Primary .data reconstruction; removed multiple incbins and expanded them into explicit words, strings, labels, and symbol pointers.
Added many global labels for function entries or branch/jump-table targets referenced from data.
Added global labels around functions and jump-table branch targets such as lbl_80067498-style labels.
Added global labels for branch targets and function boundaries referenced by reconstructed data.
Added labels needed for item-related pointer tables.
Added labels needed for item-related pointer tables.
Added labels near lb/data references; data excerpt includes lbRefSetUnuse and lbRefData-related strings.
Added func_803534BC global label for a function boundary.
Added func_80347BFC global label for a function boundary.
Added func_8034E6A0 global label for a function boundary.
Added a baselib label needed by data pointers.
Added a baselib label needed by data pointers.

what_changed: Replaced large .incbin ranges in asm/data.s with explicit data declarations. Examples include lbl_803BB0E0, lbl_803BB300, lbl_803BB3C0, lbl_803BB5D0, lbl_803BB6B0, lbl_803BB800, and lbl_803BB8D4.

Split formerly opaque data blobs into many address-named global labels such as lbl_803BB0F8, lbl_803BB110, lbl_803BB120, lbl_803BB17C, lbl_803BB194, and many lbl_803BBAxx/lbl_803BBBxx/lbl_803BBCxx labels.

Converted some embedded pointers to symbolic .4byte references, for example .4byte lbl_803BB0F8, .4byte lbl_803BB110, .4byte lbl_803BB120, .4byte lbl_803BB17C, .4byte lbl_803BB194, .4byte func_80022650, and .4byte func_80022940.

Decoded many byte sequences into visible strings or string-like word data, including lbRefSetUnuse error text, lbRefData-related data, /audio/, .ssm filename entries such as main.ssm, pokemon.ssm, nr_title.ssm, and character/stage music names.

Added global labels in code assembly files for function starts and branch-table targets, e.g. func_803534BC, func_80347BFC, func_8034E6A0, func_8002B3D4, func_8002C908, func_8005DBC0, and numerous lbl_8002A4F8/lbl_80067498-style case labels.
Introduced or exposed jump-table labels in data, including jtbl_803C6118, while leaving some remaining unknown chunks as incbin such as jtbl_803C613C and jtbl_803C61BC in the diff excerpt.
Touched 17 files total, with the context reporting 5657 additions and 248 deletions; asm/data.s accounted for most of the size with 5184 additions and 248 deletions.
smart_moves: Did the cleanup incrementally: the PR title says this is the first part, and the diff shows some unknown regions still preserved as .incbin instead of forcing uncertain interpretation.
Added global text labels before converting data pointers, which lets .data use symbols for function pointers and jump-table targets without changing code bytes.
Carved sublabels at internal data offsets before referencing them, making later struct recovery easier than one monolithic label per old incbin.
Used address-stable autogenerated names for unknown labels, minimizing semantic overclaiming while still making the data linkable and searchable.
Preserved alignment and string termination where visible, using .asciz and .balign for some strings and explicit zero words where the exact binary layout matters.
Exposed repeated pointer-table patterns and filename arrays, creating footholds for future typed C data definitions.
decomp_lessons: For .data decompilation, first replace opaque incbins with exact assembly data before attempting semantic C structs; this improves relocation visibility without requiring full type knowledge.
When a data table contains code pointers, add labels at the target functions or branch labels in the relevant .s files before changing the data words to symbolic references.
Unknown data can still be improved safely by splitting it into address labels and exact .4byte values; semantic names should wait until evidence is stronger.
Do not remove every incbin blindly. Keeping uncertain jump-table or binary regions as incbin is safer than making incorrect declarations.
String-looking data may appear either as .asciz or as packed .4byte words depending on alignment and matching needs; preserving exact layout is more important than readability.
Large data cleanup PRs benefit from being staged by address range or subsystem because they create many cross-file label dependencies.
naming_conventions: Unknown data labels use address-based lbl_XXXXXXXX names, e.g. lbl_803BB0F8 and lbl_803BBC30.
Unknown function-entry labels use func_XXXXXXXX, e.g. func_803534BC, func_80347BFC, func_8034E6A0, and func_8005DBC0.
Jump-table labels use jtbl_XXXXXXXX where identified, e.g. jtbl_803C6118.
Branch/case targets inside functions are exported as lbl_XXXXXXXX when referenced by data jump tables, e.g. lbl_8002A4F8 and lbl_80067498.
The PR mostly avoids semantic renaming; address-derived names are used until stronger evidence exists.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace .incbin "baserom.dol", offset, size with exact .4byte constants, .asciz strings, .balign directives, and new labels.
Represent known relocatable pointers as .4byte symbol instead of raw address when the target label is available.
Add .global before labels that must be referenced across assembly files or from data.s.
Expose function boundaries by inserting .global func_XXXXXXXX immediately before the existing instruction stream, without changing instructions.
Expose computed-branch case labels after bctr/jump-table dispatch sites so reconstructed jump tables can reference labels.
Leave unresolved regions as .incbin to maintain binary match when the table structure is not fully decoded.
Use NULL as an explicit .4byte value where the assembler/project convention supports it, as seen near a string block in the diff excerpt.

review_feedback.details: The provided context reports zero issue comments, zero review comments, and zero reviews. No reviewer guidance or requested changes are available.

searchable_terms: First part of cleaning up incbins/pointers in .data
follow_up_queries: Find remaining .incbin directives in asm/data.s near jtbl_803C613C and jtbl_803C61BC for later table reconstruction.
Search for raw .4byte 0x800xxxxx and .4byte 0x803xxxxx values in asm/data.s that could now be replaced with symbols.
Audit lbl_803BBA94 through lbl_803BBCF0 as a likely filename pointer table and recover a typed array if enough consumers are decompiled.
Trace references to lbRefSetUnuse, lbRefData, func_80022650, and func_80022940 to identify the surrounding data structure.
Review newly exported lbl_8002A4F8/lbl_80067498-style labels to identify jump-table switch statements for future C matching.
Look for later PRs that semantically rename or type the data ranges first exposed in PR 90.
